entry.title: My workflow for writing blog posts

link.title: My workflow for writing blog posts

entry.content:
Steps to write a post

  1. Switch to the source branch and write a new post with markdown format and save it in the _posts directory

    $ git checkout source
$ vim _posts/2018-06-23-a-new-post.md
# write a new blog post

  2. Test it locally

    $ jekyll build
$ jekyll serve

  3. Push to github

    $ rake

    As github page only support a few whitelisted jekyll plugins due to security reasons. Many useful plugins like jekyll-scholad, jekyll-katex cannot be used.

    A workaround is to build the site locally using jekyll build and push the resulting html (everything inside the _site folder) to the master branch.

    To Automate the whole process, I modified the Rakefile from David Ensinger. You can see the modified script here. This command will run jekyll build on the source branch and copy everythin in the _site directory to the master branch and push everything to github

Some Demo

Math equation using KaTeX!

Code Snippet!

def set_seed(x):
  """Set seed for both NumPy and TensorFlow.

  Parameters
  ----------
  x : int, float
    seed
  """
  np.random.seed(x)
  tf.set_random_seed(x)

var s = "JavaScript syntax highlighting";
alert(s);

Reference

Jekyll.

Markdown Cheat Sheet.
entry.summary: Steps to write a post Switch to the source branch and write a new post with markdown format and save it in the _posts directory $ git checkout source $ vim _posts/2018-06-23-a-new-post.md # write a new blog post Test it locally $ jekyll build $ jekyll serve Push to github $ rake As github page only support a few whitelisted jekyll plugins due to security reasons. Many useful plugins like jekyll-scholad, jekyll-katex cannot be used. A workaround is to build the site locally using jekyll build and push the resulting html (everything inside the _site folder) to the master branch. To Automate the whole process, I modified the Rakefile from David Ensinger. You can see the modified script here. This command will run jekyll build on the source branch and copy everythin in the _site directory to the master branch and push everything to github Some Demo Math equation using KaTeX! Code Snippet! def set_seed(x): """Set seed for both NumPy and TensorFlow. Parameters ---------- x : int, float seed """ np.random.seed(x) tf.set_random_seed(x) var s = "JavaScript syntax highlighting"; alert(s); Reference Jekyll. Markdown Cheat Sheet.
